### Top reviewed [...] Raw data vs. transformations - we started as a raw data pipe, but we've been building on top of it. Today we already calculate ROAS for Apple Ads, real ASO organic downloads, revenue per download, and we pull in metadata like app titles and icons to make dashboards easier to build. With the AI layer, you can also derive basically any custom metric you need on the fly.

Long-term vision - reliable data piping stays the foundation. But the direction is making the data come to you, not the other way around. We started with Looker Studio, added BigQuery, now AI. Next up: mobile app, Chrome extension, and smarter alerts. The goal is that you never have to go looking for your data.

Thanks for the thoughtful questions! Did I reply it correctly? Let me know if I missed anything. [...] Easy App Reports

@denis\_andrade yes, you can get now only your raw data but also pre-built metrics such as Apple Ads ROAS and true iOS organic downloads to name a couple.  
  
There are multiple ways you can extract your data from Easy App Reports:  
  
- With Google BigQuery: we save all datasets and tables on YOUR BigQuery project, not ours (Pro Plan or higher)  
- Native BigQuery to Sheets: your data can be automatically sent to Sheets too if you prefer that  
- Via our API: extract your data and store it wherever you want  
  
So you don't ever get locked with us. If you ever need to leave, you can take your data with you. Also, we're GDPR and CCPA compliant so we always respect our customer's right to forget if needed.  
  
Hope to see you onboard soon!

Yuri Moreno

#### What's great

I like Metabase, we've used it in the company I worked at for a while, and as a BI analyst I get most of my day-to-day tasks done there. As a non-technical user it saves lots of time as I don't rely on devs for SQL and tasks that would otherwise take much time (e.g. visualization), are done quicker. It is also great for cross-departmental communication.

#### What needs improvement [...] #### What needs improvement

Despite its growth and (I would assume) huge budgets going into understanding what their customers really want, the exporting dashboards and their customization elements are very hard to navigate. What I mean is that, just like everything else is optimized for simplicity, so should data visualization be as well. Perhaps a tool that you can have a look at that does a great job and is similar to Metabase is TalkBI. You simply click on one button and poof - report is made. Other than that, perhaps it would be possible to review the SQL generated by Metabase so we can help improve its effectiveness for more complex queries.
